scenarios.description: children() returns empty Elements when element has no child nodes (childNodeSize == 0)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); // no children

scenarios.description: children() returns empty Elements when only non-element child nodes exist (filterNodes finds zero Element instances)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendText("text"); // one TextNode child
pseudocode: TEST TC02 children only non-element children

scenarios.description: children() returns single-element list when exactly one Element child exists
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); Element child = new Element("span"); parent.appendChild(child);
pseudocode: TEST TC03 children one element child
Element parent = new Element("div"); Element child = new Element("span"); parent.appendChild(child);

scenarios.description: children() returns multiple elements in correct order when mixed children exist
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("ul"); Element li1 = new Element("li"); Element li2 = new Element("li"); TextNode t = new TextNode("x"); parent.appendChild(li1); parent.appendChild(t); parent.appendChild(li2);
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements.size() == 2 and order is [li1, li2]
pseudocode: TEST TC04 children multiple element and non-element

Element parent = new Element("ul"); Element li1 = new Element("li"); Element li2 = new Element("li"); TextNode t = new TextNode("x"); parent.appendChild(li1); parent.appendChild(t); parent.appendChild(li2);
assert result.size() == 2 && result.get(0) == li1 && result.get(1) == li2;
